vision.title: Control9 Pipeline Integrations
vision.mission: Make Control9 installable in customer CI/CD systems with a small, trustworthy GitHub Action and GitLab CI component that govern infrastructure changes without becoming the customer's execution platform.
vision.core_purpose: This repo owns the pipeline edge: collecting CDK/CloudFormation and Terraform/OpenTofu context, redacting sensitive data, building signed action envelopes, calling the Control9 policy API, posting results back to workflows, and verifying deploy-time fingerprints before production authority is used.
vision.key_architecture: A public integration surface for GitHub Actions and GitLab CI/CD. The components run in client repositories and CI/CD runners, classify commands and IaC artifacts, create stable action envelopes, submit summaries to the Control9 control plane, receive allow, deny, approval-required, or observe decisions, and update checks, comments, or jobs accordingly.

strategic_goals.short_term: Ship the GitHub shadow-mode path: repo config, Terraform plan parser, CDK synth/diff parser, signed envelope submission, observe-only response handling, redaction defaults, and PR check summary without changing the deploy path.
strategic_goals.medium_term: Add enforce-mode integrations: approval waits, deploy-step verification, protected environment support, GitLab CI component/templates, merge request comments, monorepo support, and richer environment and artifact identity.
strategic_goals.long_term: Support enterprise estates with mixed GitHub/GitLab usage, customer-hosted or SaaS endpoints, optional customer-owned evidence storage, and integration patterns that remain easy to audit and install.

key_principles.text: Stay small at the customer edge. The integration should send signed, redacted envelopes and summaries to Control9, not become a local policy engine with hidden product logic.
Default to shadow mode for first installation so customers can see risk output without blocking deploys.
Protect secrets before data leaves the client boundary. Redact raw tokens, private keys, environment values, and sensitive command output by default.
Deploy verification is a first-class path: the plan, template, or artifact fingerprint approved by Control9 must match what the deploy step is about to use.
The integration reports decisions where developers already work: GitHub checks, PR comments, GitLab job status, and merge request comments.

competitive_advantages.text: A public, reviewable install surface lowers trust friction for customers who need to place Control9 inside production delivery pipelines.
Native GitHub and GitLab support lets Control9 govern approved paths without asking teams to abandon their CI/CD provider.

differentiation_from_competitors.text: Unlike remote IaC execution platforms, these integrations leave plan, synth, diff, and deploy work in the customer's pipeline.
Unlike plain CI approvals, the integration ties the decision to a signed envelope and artifact fingerprint that the SaaS can record and verify.
